concept: The hammer and the anvil — heat as a tool, not a punishment
quote: Do not pray for less heat. Pray for a heavier hammer.

visual_prompt.primary_prompt: Dark cinematic forge of identity. A calm figure bathed in furnace glow is reshaped by warm heat, rhythmic hammer falls, molten gold light, glowing embers, dark polished steel. High contrast, stoic, mythic, no readable text.

script.full_text: You think the fire is trying to break you. The fire is just making you soft enough to be reshaped. Without the heat, you are just a useless slab of iron. The pain is the craftsman. The pressure is the blueprint. Stop asking for the furnace to cool down. You were born to be forged. Stand still, and let the hammer finish its work.

segments.text: The fire is shaping you. Without the heat, you are just a useless slab of iron. The pressure is the blueprint.
segments.visual_prompt: Dark forge interior. Calm figure resting on anvil, bathed in furnace glow. Rhythmic hammer falls in slow motion. Glowing embers drift outward. Skin warms into molten gold metal. Slow cinematic push-in, high contrast.

segments.text: Let the furnace finish its work. You were born to be forged.
segments.visual_prompt: Close-up on calm molten orange eyes. Heat shimmer over polished steel skin. Hammer descends gently; embers scatter outward. Figure rises as a serene blackened warrior with gold seams. Still final frame, mythic, disciplined.
